headline: Physical Education Reduced to 1 Period Weekly in 65% of Schools
context: Despite MOE policy requiring 2 periods, most schools allocate only 1 period for physical education due to exam preparation pressure.

cards.big: 65% of schools allocate only 1 PE period weekly instead of mandated 2 periods
cards.sub: Schools divert PE time to academic subjects especially during exam preparation

cards.big: Childhood obesity in Malaysia reached 32% in 2025 the highest in Southeast Asia
cards.sub: Reduced physical activity in schools contributes to rising obesity rates

cards.big: MOE has no enforcement mechanism for minimum PE time allocation in school timetables
cards.sub: Schools face no consequences for reducing or eliminating physical education

cards.big: WHO recommends 60 minutes daily physical activity for children aged 5-17 years
cards.sub: Single 40-minute PE period meets less than 10% of weekly recommendation

cards.big: Sacrificing physical education for exam prep trades long-term health for short-term scores
Should MOE audit and enforce minimum physical education requirements in all schools
